name: TTL Enforcement
description: Pure ES6 module `static/js/ttl.js` implementing time-to-live stamping, expiry checking, and rebroadcast probability decay for the HermesP2P protocol. No dependencies on other project modules. Uses `Date.now()` for current time. Fail-safe design: malformed messages are treated as expired (dropped), and edge cases in rebroadcast probability return 0.0 (no rebroadcast).

types.name: MessageType
types.description: The three recognized message types that determine default TTL values.

types.name: Timestamp
types.description: A timestamp value accepted by parseTimestamp. Either an ISO 8601 date string or an epoch millisecond integer.

types.name: DefaultTTLs

fields.name: direct
fields.description: TTL for direct messages: 300 seconds (5 minutes).

fields.name: private
fields.description: TTL for private/group messages: 3600 seconds (1 hour).

fields.name: public
fields.description: TTL for public broadcast messages: 86400 seconds (24 hours).

types.description: Constant lookup object mapping MessageType to default TTL in seconds. Defined at module top level as DEFAULT_TTLS.

types.name: TTLMessage

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Message creation time as ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds.

fields.name: ttl
fields.description: Original total time-to-live in seconds, as stamped at creation time.

types.description: The minimal shape of a message object required by isExpired, getRemainingTTL, and getRebroadcastProbability. Must contain a timestamp and a ttl field.

types.name: MessagePackage

fields.name: ttl
fields.description: Time-to-live in seconds. Added by stampTTL, may not be present before stamping.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Message creation timestamp. Expected to be set before or after stampTTL in the pipeline.

types.description: A mutable message package object passed through the send pipeline. stampTTL adds/overwrites the `ttl` field on this object. May contain arbitrary additional fields.

types.name: BaseRebroadcastProbability
types.description: Module-level constant BASE_REBROADCAST_PROBABILITY = 0.6. The maximum rebroadcast probability when a message has its full TTL remaining.

functions.name: parseTimestamp
functions.description: Parses a timestamp value into epoch milliseconds. Accepts either a number (returned as-is, assumed to be epoch ms) or a string (parsed via `new Date(ts).getTime()`). Exported for direct use and testing. Throws on unparseable input to surface malformed data early.

inputs.name: ts
inputs.description: Timestamp to parse. Either an ISO 8601 date string or an epoch millisecond number.

error_cases.name: invalid_timestamp_type
error_cases.condition: ts is not a number and not a string (e.g., null, undefined, object, boolean)

error_data.message: Invalid timestamp: expected number or string

error_cases.name: unparseable_string
error_cases.condition: ts is a string but `new Date(ts).getTime()` returns NaN

preconditions: ts is not null and not undefined
postconditions: Return value is a finite number representing epoch milliseconds
Return value is not NaN

functions.name: getDefaultTTL
functions.description: Returns the default TTL in seconds for a given message type. Looks up the type in the DEFAULT_TTLS constant object. Throws for unknown types to surface integration bugs immediately.

validators.error_message: messageType must be one of: direct, private, public

postconditions: Return value is a positive integer
Return value is one of: 300, 3600, 86400
getDefaultTTL('direct') === 300
getDefaultTTL('private') === 3600
getDefaultTTL('public') === 86400

functions.name: isExpired
functions.description: Checks whether a message has exceeded its TTL. Computes age as `(Date.now() - parseTimestamp(message.timestamp)) / 1000`, clamps age to 0 for clock-skew safety (future timestamps), and returns `age >= message.ttl`. Fail-safe: returns true (treat as expired / drop) if timestamp or ttl is missing, invalid, or unparseable.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Message object with timestamp and ttl fields.

error_cases.name: malformed_message_failsafe
error_cases.condition: message is missing timestamp or ttl, or parseTimestamp throws, or ttl is not a positive number

error_data.behavior: Returns true (fail-safe: treat malformed messages as expired)

postconditions: Always returns a boolean, never throws
Returns true if message.timestamp or message.ttl is missing or invalid (fail-safe drop)
Returns true if computed age >= message.ttl
Returns false if computed age < message.ttl
Future timestamps (negative raw age) are clamped to age=0, so message is not expired

functions.name: getRemainingTTL
functions.description: Returns the number of seconds remaining before the message expires, clamped to 0. Computes as `Math.max(0, message.ttl - age)` where age = `(Date.now() - parseTimestamp(message.timestamp)) / 1000`, with age also clamped to 0 for clock-skew safety.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Message object with timestamp and ttl fields.

error_cases.name: invalid_timestamp
error_cases.condition: message.timestamp cannot be parsed by parseTimestamp

error_cases.name: missing_ttl
error_cases.condition: message.ttl is undefined, null, or not a number

preconditions: message.timestamp is a valid Timestamp parseable by parseTimestamp
postconditions: Return value >= 0
Return value <= message.ttl
If isExpired(message) is true, return value is 0
If message has a future timestamp, return value equals message.ttl (age clamped to 0)

functions.name: getRebroadcastProbability
functions.description: Computes the probability that this message should be rebroadcast to peers. Uses linear decay from BASE_REBROADCAST_PROBABILITY (0.6) based on remaining TTL fraction: `0.6 * (remainingTTL / message.ttl)`. Fail-safe: returns 0.0 for any edge case (expired, ttl<=0, invalid data) to prevent unnecessary rebroadcasts.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Message object with timestamp and ttl fields.

error_cases.name: any_error_failsafe
error_cases.condition: Any exception during computation (invalid timestamp, missing ttl, division by zero, etc.)

error_data.behavior: Returns 0.0 (fail-safe: do not rebroadcast on error)

postconditions: Return value >= 0.0
Return value <= 0.6 (BASE_REBROADCAST_PROBABILITY)
If message.ttl <= 0, return value is 0.0
If isExpired(message), return value is 0.0
If full TTL remaining (freshly created message), return value is 0.6
If half TTL remaining, return value is approximately 0.3
Never throws — always returns a number (fail-safe 0.0 on any error)

functions.name: stampTTL
functions.description: Adds the `ttl` field to a message package object based on the message type. Mutates the package in-place by setting `messagePackage.ttl = getDefaultTTL(messageType)`. Returns the same object reference for pipeline chaining. Used in the send pipeline: compose -> encrypt -> stampTTL -> pseudonym -> package -> sign -> serialize -> pad -> broadcast.

inputs.name: messagePackage
inputs.description: The message package object to stamp. Mutated in-place.

inputs.name: messageType
inputs.description: One of 'direct', 'private', or 'public'. Determines the TTL value.

error_data.message: Propagates Error from getDefaultTTL: Unknown message type

postconditions: Return value is the same object reference as messagePackage (===)
messagePackage.ttl is set to getDefaultTTL(messageType)
All other fields on messagePackage are preserved unchanged
If messagePackage already had a ttl field, it is overwritten

structured_side_effects.description: Sets messagePackage.ttl to the default TTL for the given message type

invariants: DEFAULT_TTLS is a frozen constant: { direct: 300, private: 3600, public: 86400 }
All functions are pure with respect to external state (only Date.now() is read from environment)
No function logs private keys, secret keys, or decrypted message content
isExpired and getRebroadcastProbability never throw — they return fail-safe values (true and 0.0 respectively)
parseTimestamp and getDefaultTTL throw on invalid input to surface bugs early
Age is always clamped to >= 0 to handle clock skew (future timestamps)
Rebroadcast probability decays linearly from 0.6 to 0.0 as remaining TTL decreases
Module has zero dependencies on other project modules — it is a pure leaf component
All exports are named exports; no default export
